LabourRecord

Tamil Nadu statutory compliance manager for hospitals and shops. Replaces paper registers with digital data entry and generates all required government forms.

Clinical Establishments Act Tamil Nadu Shops & Establishments Act Form XI · XII · XVII · IV · I · II Form U · W · T · X

1 Overview

LabourRecord helps you maintain all statutory records required by Tamil Nadu labour law for hospitals (Clinical Establishments Act) and shops/offices (Shops & Establishments Act). Enter data once — attendance, wages, overtime, leave — and the app generates every required government register automatically.

Fig 1.1 — Dashboard showing navigation sidebar with all modules

For Hospitals

FormDescription
Form XIRegister of Employees
Form VMuster Roll (Attendance)
Form XIIRegister of Wages
Form XVIIWage Slips
Form IVOvertime Muster Roll
Form IRegister of Fines
Form IIRegister of Deductions

For Shops

FormDescription
Form UEmployee Register
Form VRegister of Employment
Form WRegister of Wages
Form TWage Slips
Form XLeave & Social Security

2 First-Time Setup Flow

Always follow this order when setting up a new establishment for the first time:

1 Create Establishment (hospital or shop)
↓
2 Add Employees (with wage defaults)
↓
3 Add Government Holidays (optional)
↓
4 Configure Wage Rules (optional)
↓
5 Create Monthly Cycle
↓
6 Enter Attendance / Wages / OT / Leave
↓
7 Print Salary Slips & Government Forms
Tip Steps 3 and 4 are optional — the system applies sensible Tamil Nadu defaults (Sundays off, statutory PF/ESI rates). You only need to configure these if your establishment has different rules.

3 Starting the Application

Open Terminal, navigate to the app folder, and run:

cd ~/labour-record/labour-record-app
npm run dev

Then open http://localhost:3000 in your browser. The app loads with the dashboard as shown in Fig 1.1 above.

Production use For daily office use, run npm run build once, then use npm start instead of npm run dev. Production mode is faster and more stable.

4 Establishments

An establishment is a hospital or shop. Everything in the system — employees, cycles, forms — belongs to one establishment. Create your establishment first before adding anything else.

Fig 4.1 — Establishments list. Each row shows the establishment type, and Edit/Delete actions.

Creating a New Establishment

Click + New Establishment on the Establishments page.
Fill in the Establishment Name — the legal registered name, e.g. "DNV Orthocare". Minimum 3 characters.
Enter the full Address including city and pincode. This appears on all printed forms.
Enter Employer Name (owner/proprietor) and Manager/In-Charge name.
Enter the Registration Certificate No. — e.g. TN-HR-2021-001
Select Type: Hospital for clinical establishments, Shop for shops/offices. This controls which set of government forms is generated.
Review Wage Formula Configuration — set Fixed Allowance (hospitals), LWF Rate, and whether ESI applies. Then click Create Establishment.

Fig 4.2 — New establishment form. The ⓘ icon on each field shows a tooltip with an example when hovered.

Wage Formula Configuration Fields

FieldHospitalShopExample
PresetTN_MINIMUM_WAGES_HOSPITALTN_SHOPS_ESTABLISHMENTSAuto-set by Type
Fixed Allowance (₹)✓ applicable—₹360/month per TN notification
HRA (₹)—✓ applicable₹500/month
LWF Rate✓✓₹0.25 (TN standard)
ESI ApplicableIf any employee ≤ ₹21,000 grossIf any employee ≤ ₹21,000 grossCheck if applicable
Cannot delete an establishment? Deletion is blocked if the establishment has employees or monthly cycles. Delete the cycles first, then the employees, then the establishment.

5 Employees

Employees are linked to one establishment. The easiest way to add an employee is from the Establishment detail page — the establishment is then pre-selected automatically.

Fig 5.1 — Employee list filtered by establishment. Shows Emp ID, name, designation, and status badge.

Adding an Employee

Navigate to Establishments → click your establishment → + Add Employee. The establishment field will be pre-filled and shown as read-only.

Fig 5.2 — New employee form. The establishment is pre-selected when accessed from the establishment detail page.

Required Fields

Personal Details

FieldNotes
Employee IDUnique per establishment, e.g. H001
Full NameAs per identity document
SexM / F / Other
Date of BirthMust be ≥ 14 yrs before Date of Entry
Father/Spouse NameAppears on Form XI
Present AddressCurrent address
Permanent AddressPermanent address

Statutory & Wage Details

FieldNotes
UAN12-digit PF account number
ESI NoESI insurance number
IFSC CodeFormat: SBIN0001234
Basic Wage⚠ Monthly basic, e.g. ₹6,000
DA⚠ Dearness allowance, e.g. ₹1,360
HRAShops only
Important — Wage Defaults Always fill in Basic Wage and DA. If left as ₹0, the wages tab in monthly cycles will show zero for this employee, and you will see a ⚠ warning. You can update these later by editing the employee.

Validation Rules

The form enforces these rules before saving:

  • Age must be ≥ 14 years (Date of Birth vs Date of Entry)
  • Date of Exit must be after Date of Entry
  • Exit Reason is required if Date of Exit is filled
  • Mobile number: exactly 10 digits
  • UAN: exactly 12 digits
  • IFSC: format ABCD0123456
  • Basic Wage cannot exceed ₹2,00,000/month

6 Government Holidays

Government holidays affect two things automatically: (1) attendance marks — holiday dates are set to H by default when a form is first opened; (2) wages — employees who work on a holiday earn double wages (controlled by the Holiday Multiplier rule).

Fig 6.1 — Holidays management page. Add Tamil Nadu government holidays by date and name.

Adding a Holiday

Click Add Holiday.
Select the Date from the date picker. Must be within ±3 years of today.
Enter the Holiday Name, e.g. "Pongal", "Republic Day", "Tamil New Year". Minimum 3 characters.
Click Add. The holiday appears in the list immediately.
Add holidays before creating cycles Holiday attendance defaults (H marks) are applied only when a form task is opened for the first time. Holidays added after a form has been opened will not retroactively update saved attendance.

Common Tamil Nadu Government Holidays to Add

DateHoliday
Jan 14Pongal
Jan 26Republic Day
Apr 14Tamil New Year / Dr. Ambedkar Jayanti
Aug 15Independence Day
Oct 2Gandhi Jayanti
Nov 1Tamil Nadu Day
Dec 25Christmas

7 Wage Rules

Wage rules define PF/ESI percentages, overtime multiplier, and holiday pay multiplier. Each establishment can override the system defaults. The right panel shows a live salary slip simulator — changes update instantly as you edit.

Fig 7.1 — Wage Rules page before selecting an establishment. Select from the dropdown to load rules.

Fig 7.2 — Wage Rules loaded for DNV Orthocare. Left: rules table with Edit buttons. Right: live salary slip simulator reacts instantly to any rule edit.

System Default Values

RuleDefaultMeaning
HOLIDAY_MULTIPLIER2.0×Double wages for working on a government holiday
OT_MULTIPLIER2.0×Double hourly rate for overtime hours
PF_EMPLOYEE_PCT12%Employee PF deduction (% of Basic wage)
PF_EMPLOYER_PCT13%Employer PF contribution (% of Basic wage)
ESI_EMPLOYEE_PCT0.75%Employee ESI deduction (% of Gross wages)
ESI_EMPLOYER_PCT3.25%Employer ESI contribution (% of Gross wages)

Editing a Rule

Select an establishment from the dropdown at the top.
Click Edit on the rule you want to change. The row turns green and an input field appears.
On the right panel, the affected salary slip line highlights in green — showing exactly what will change.
Enter the new value and click Save. A blue value in the table means a custom override is active.
Live Simulator Adjust the simulator inputs (Monthly Basic, DA, wage days, holiday days, OT hours) on the right panel to see how pay is computed for any scenario — useful for answering employee questions about their salary breakdown.

8 Monthly Cycles

A monthly cycle is one payroll period — one month, one establishment. It snapshots active employees at creation time and creates all required form tasks automatically.

Fig 8.1 — Monthly Cycles list. Each row shows the establishment, period, employee count, and a Delete option.

Creating a Cycle

Click + New Cycle.
Select the Establishment.
Select the Month and Year.
Set Wage Period Days — the number of working days in the month. Example: 26 for a 6-day week; 25 if 1 holiday fell on a working day.
Click Create Cycle. All active employees are snapped into the cycle. Form tasks are created automatically.

Fig 8.2 — Cycle detail page. Top: Form Tasks with Open/Print/Slips links and status badges. Bottom: employee list with Sync Employees and Salary Slips buttons.

Form Task Statuses

NOT STARTED DATA ENTRY READY FOR REVIEW NEEDS CORRECTION APPROVED EXPORTED

Syncing New Employees

If you add an employee to the establishment after creating a cycle, they won't appear in the cycle automatically. Click Sync Employees on the cycle detail page to pull them in.

Deleting a cycle is permanent Deleting a cycle removes all its data — attendance, wages, leave, OT, fines, deductions, and generated documents. This cannot be undone.

9 Attendance Entry

Open a cycle → click Open on any form task → the Attendance tab is shown first.

Fig 9.1 — Attendance tab. Each row is an employee; each column is a day of the month. Click any cell to cycle through attendance codes.

Attendance Codes

CodeMeaningCounts As
PPresentDays Worked + 1
AAbsentAbsent Days + 1
HHoliday / Weekly OffNot counted in wages
OTPresent with OvertimeDays Worked + 1
LOn LeaveLeave Days + 1 (wage days counted)
P
P
H
P
OT
P
P
H
A
P
P
L
P

Example attendance strip: P=Present, H=Holiday/Sunday, OT=Overtime, A=Absent, L=Leave

Default Marks (applied automatically on first open)

  • Monday – Saturday → P (Present)
  • Sunday → H (Holiday)
  • Government holiday dates → H (overrides the above)

Wage Days Formula

Wage Days  = Days Worked (P + OT) + Leave Days (L)
Days Worked = number of P + OT marks
Absent Days = number of A marks   (no wages paid)

After editing, click Save Attendance for each employee row.

10 Wages Entry

Click the Wages tab in the form entry page. Wages are pre-populated automatically from the employee's wage defaults, prorated by their actual wage days.

Proration Formula

Prorated Basic = Monthly Basic × Wage Days ÷ Days in Month
Prorated DA    = Monthly DA    × Wage Days ÷ Days in Month

Example (26 wage days, 30-day month, ₹6,000 basic):
  Prorated Basic = 6000 × 26 ÷ 30 = ₹5,200

PF  = Prorated Basic × PF Employee %  (e.g. 5200 × 12% = ₹624)
ESI = Gross Wages   × ESI Employee %  (e.g. 7360 × 0.75% = ₹55.20)

Editable Fields per Employee

FieldNotes
Basic, DA, HRAAuto-prorated; can override
PF, ESI, LWFAuto-calculated; can override
Other AllowancesFree-form additional amounts
Advance RecoveredDeduct salary advance given earlier
Other DeductionsAny other deduction
Payment DateDate wages were paid
Receipt ReferenceCheque/transfer reference number
⚠ Zero-wage warning If you see a yellow warning banner, one or more employees have Basic = ₹0. Their wage defaults were not set. Click the "Set wage defaults" link in the warning to go to the employee profile and fill in the monthly Basic and DA values.

Click Save Wages. Validation checks that days worked is in range and no field is negative.

11 Overtime

Click the Overtime tab. Enter OT hours per employee per day in the grid. The system calculates total OT earnings automatically.

OT Calculation Formula

Daily Rate  = (Prorated Basic + DA) ÷ Wage Days
Hourly Rate = Daily Rate ÷ 8
OT Earnings = Hourly Rate × OT Multiplier × Total OT Hours

Example (₹5,200 basic, ₹1,170 DA, 26 wage days, 4 OT hours):
  Daily Rate  = (5200 + 1170) ÷ 26 = ₹245
  Hourly Rate = 245 ÷ 8 = ₹30.63
  OT Earnings = 30.63 × 2.0 × 4 = ₹245

Click Save Overtime after entering hours.

12 Fines & Deductions

The Fines and Deductions tabs work similarly. Each entry is a separate record — one offence or one damage incident = one row.

Adding a Fine (Form I — Register of Fines)

Click the Fines tab.
Select the Employee from the dropdown.
Enter Offence Date, Offence Description (what the employee did), and Fine Amount.
Optionally enter Amount Recovered this month and any Remarks.
Click Add Fine. To delete a row, click the ✕ button.

Adding a Deduction (Form II — Register of Deductions)

Same process as fines, but for damage/loss to employer property. Fields: Date of Damage, Description, Deduction Amount, Amount Recovered, Remarks.

Tip Fine and deduction amounts entered here are automatically carried into the Wages tab as Fine Deduction and Other Deductions, reducing the employee's net pay.

13 Leave Balances

Click the Leave tab to enter leave balances for the month. The Earned Leave closing balance is calculated automatically.

Fig 13.1 — Leave tab showing earned leave opening/during/availed fields and auto-calculated closing balance per employee.

FieldMeaningExample
EL OpeningEarned leave balance at start of month5 days
Earned DuringLeave accrued this month (1 day per 20 days worked)1 day
EL AvailedLeave taken this month2 days
EL ClosingAuto: Opening + Earned − Availed (min 0)4 days
Medical LeaveMedical/sick leave days this month0
Other LeaveAny other approved leave0

Click Save Leave. This data populates Form X (shops) or the leave register.

14 Salary Slips

From the cycle detail page, click Salary Slips to view and print salary slips for all employees in the cycle.

Fig 14.1 — Salary Slips grid. Each card shows earnings, deductions, and net pay for one employee. View/Print buttons appear at the bottom of each card.

Individual Slip View

Click View on any card to open a full-page, print-ready salary slip for that employee.

Fig 14.2 — Individual salary slip. Shows all earnings, deductions, net pay, and signature lines. Navigate between employees with the ← → buttons.

Printing Options

ButtonWhat it does
Print All (top-right on grid)Prints all slips in a 2-column layout. Sidebar is hidden automatically.
Print (on each grid card)Opens the individual slip in a new tab and triggers the print dialog immediately.
Print Slip (on individual slip page)Prints only that one slip. Sidebar and navigation are hidden.
Tip — distribute slips Use Print on each card to print one slip at a time on individual sheets. Use Print All to print the full batch for record-keeping.

15 Printing Government Forms

From the cycle detail page, click Print next to any form task. The form opens in a new browser tab as a print-ready page.

Fig 15.1 — Print view of Form XI (Register of Employees). Press Cmd+P (Mac) or Ctrl+P (Windows) to print or save as PDF.

All Available Forms

Hospital Forms

FormRuleDescription
Form XIRule 25Register of Employees
Form VRule 26Muster Roll
Form XIIRule 27(1)Register of Wages
Form XVIIRule 27(3)Wage Slips
Form IVRule 28Overtime Register
Form IRule 72(1)Register of Fines
Form IIRule 72(2)Register of Deductions

Shop Forms

FormRuleDescription
Form URule 16Employee Register
Form VRule 17Register of Employment
Form WRule 18Register of Wages
Form TRule 19Wage Slips
Form XRule 20Leave & Social Security
Save as PDF In the browser print dialog, choose Save as PDF as the destination to get a PDF copy for records or email.

16 Troubleshooting

ProblemSolution
Wages showing ₹0 for an employee Go to Employees → edit employee → fill Basic Wage and DA. These must be set before wages auto-populate.
Employee missing from cycle Employee was added after cycle creation. Go to cycle detail → Sync Employees.
Cannot delete establishment The establishment has employees or cycles. Delete cycles first, then employees, then the establishment.
Government holiday not marked as H Add the holiday in the Holidays page before opening the form task for the first time. Existing saved attendance is not retroactively changed.
Print shows the sidebar Use the Print Slip button on the individual slip page, or the Print link on the cycle detail. Do not use Cmd+P directly on the main app pages.
App not loading at localhost:3000 Open Terminal and run:
cd ~/labour-record/labour-record-app && npm run dev
Build fails after install Check Node.js version: node --version must be v20+. Re-run bash ~/labour-record/man/install.sh.
Database appears empty after moving machine Ensure the prisma/dev.db file was copied along with the rest of the labour-record-app/ folder.
